title: Tamil Era Thokku (Prawn Stir-Fry)

subs.name: 500 g squid (kanava), cut into rings — add 3 extra minutes of cooking
coconut oil or neutral oil
subs.notes: gingelly oil gives the coastal Tamil flavour

subs.name: a small piece of tamarind dissolved in 1 tbsp warm water

steps.text: Toss the cleaned prawns with a pinch of turmeric, chilli powder and salt. Set aside while you prepare the masala base.

Heat gingelly oil in a kadai on medium-high. Splutter the mustard seeds, add fennel seeds and curry leaves — they will pop and crackle. Add the chopped shallots and fry until they are lightly golden, about 4 minutes.

Add ginger-garlic paste and fry for 1 minute. Add the tomatoes and cook on medium-high heat, pressing and stirring, until the tomatoes completely dissolve into the masala and the oil separates — about 5 minutes. The base should be thick and pasty.
Add the remaining red chilli powder and coriander powder. Stir for 30 seconds on low flame, then raise the heat to high and add the prawns. Toss to coat thoroughly in the masala.

Stir-fry on high heat for 4–5 minutes until the prawns are pink and curled and the masala clings tightly to each piece. The edges of the mixture should begin to caramelise. Do not overcook — overcooked prawns turn rubbery.
Squeeze in lemon juice, scatter coriander leaves and toss once more. Serve hot as a dry side with sambar rice or as an appetiser with sliced onion and lemon.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
safety_warnings: Keep raw animal products, their plates and utensils separate from cooked food; wash hands and surfaces after contact.
critical_checks: Cook seafood until safely done for the species and preparation; keep it chilled before cooking.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.
trust.substitution_warning: A substitution can introduce allergens or change dietary suitability. Never claim a swap is safe without checking the replacement's label and the user's allergy context.

substitution_notes: prawn → 500 g squid (kanava), cut into rings — add 3 extra minutes of cooking; gingelly-oil → coconut oil or neutral oil (gingelly oil gives the coastal Tamil flavour); shallot → 1 medium onion, finely chopped; lemon → a small piece of tamarind dissolved in 1 tbsp warm water
